title: Fagraea lanceolata
taxonomy: Fagraea lanceolata is classified in the family Loganiaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical Asia and is a member of the genus Fagraea. The Loganiaceae family is a small group of flowering plants with around 100 species worldwide. Fagraea lanceolata is a woody climber with fragrant white flowers that are highly scented at night. This plant is often found in tropical forests and is valued for its unique flowers and climbing habit.

intro: Fagraea lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Fagraea lanceolata include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities.
